user_query:
We’re preparing a compliance spot-check for a small agent workspace snapshot. Please do the following using only the files under input/ and write all outputs under output/:

1) Create a security policy file at output/.marshal-policy.json with this exact structure (JSON):
- version: 1
- name: "default"
- rules:
  - commands:
    - allow: ["git", "python3", "node", "npm", "pip"]
    - block: ["curl|bash", "wget -O-|sh", "rm -rf /", "chmod 777"]
    - review: ["sudo", "docker", "ssh"]
  - network:
    - allow_domains: ["github.com", "pypi.org", "npmjs.com"]
    - block_domains: ["pastebin.com", "transfer.sh", "ngrok.io"]
    - block_patterns: ["*.tk", "*.ml", "*.ga"]
  - data_handling:
    - pii_scan: true
    - secret_scan: true
    - log_retention_days: 90
  - workspace:
    - require_gitignore: true
    - require_audit_trail: false     (set to false for this task)
    - require_skill_signing: false   (set to false for this task)
    - max_skill_risk_score: 50

2) Perform a static audit of the workspace at input/WORKSPACE (treat input/WORKSPACE as the workspace root). Scan only code files with extensions .py, .sh, .bash, .zsh, .js, .ts inside input/WORKSPACE/skills/** (recursively). Use these exact rule names, severities, and detection criteria:
- commands.pipe_to_shell (CRITICAL): any line containing both "curl" and a pipe "|" leading to "bash" on the same line.
- commands.rm_rf_root (CRITICAL): any line containing the literal substring "rm -rf /".
- commands.chmod_777 (CRITICAL): any line containing the literal substring "chmod 777".
- network.block_domains (CRITICAL): any HTTP(S) URL whose domain is one of ["pastebin.com", "transfer.sh", "ngrok.io"].
- network.block_patterns (HIGH): any HTTP(S) URL whose domain ends with one of [".tk", ".ml", ".ga"].
- network.allow_domains (INFO): if an HTTP(S) URL’s domain is not in ["github.com", "pypi.org", "npmjs.com"], flag it as informational.
- data_handling.secret_scan (HIGH): because policy requires secret_scan=true, if there is no skill directory named exactly "openclaw-sentry" under input/WORKSPACE/skills, add one HIGH-severity finding.
- data_handling.pii_scan (MEDIUM): because policy requires pii_scan=true, if there is no PII scanner installed (same "openclaw-sentry" assumption for this task), add one MEDIUM-severity finding.
- workspace.require_gitignore (MEDIUM): because policy requires a .gitignore, if input/WORKSPACE/.gitignore is missing, add one MEDIUM-severity finding.
- config.debug_mode (LOW): any line containing a truthy debug flag like "DEBUG = True" or "debug = True" (case-insensitive for the variable name, exact "True").
- config.debug_print (LOW): any line that prints a debug tag, e.g., contains print(...) with the substring "DEBUG" inside the string literal.

Details for detection:
- Treat line numbers as 1-based. Each matching line counts as a separate finding (no de-duplication across rules).
- For network rules, detect domains from any substring starting with http:// or https:// and continue until the next whitespace or quote. The domain is the host portion between the scheme and first slash, ignoring leading/trailing quotes.
- For findings from source files, set the "file" to the path relative to the workspace root, e.g., "skills/<skill-name>/<file>".
- For the two data_handling findings and the workspace.require_gitignore finding, set file and line as follows:
  - data_handling.secret_scan: file ".marshal-policy.json", line 0
  - data_handling.pii_scan: file ".marshal-policy.json", line 0
  - workspace.require_gitignore: file ".gitignore", line 0

3) Scoring and grading:
- Start at score = 100.
- Deduct per finding: CRITICAL 25, HIGH 15, MEDIUM 8, LOW 3, INFO 1.
- Floor the final score at 0.
- Grade map: A: 90-100, B: 75-89, C: 60-74, D: 40-59, F: 0-39.
- Exit code: 0 if no findings, 1 if any MEDIUM/HIGH but no CRITICAL, 2 if any CRITICAL.

4) Write two audit outputs:
- output/audit.json with this structure:
  {
    "policy_path": "./.marshal-policy.json",
    "findings": [
      {"rule": "<one of the rule names above>", "severity": "CRITICAL|HIGH|MEDIUM|LOW|INFO", "file": "<relative path>", "line": <int>, "description": "<short text>"},
      ...
    ],
    "counts": {"CRITICAL": <int>, "HIGH": <int>, "MEDIUM": <int>, "LOW": <int>, "INFO": <int>},
    "total_deductions": <int>,
    "score": <int>,
    "grade": "A|B|C|D|F",
    "exit_code": 0|1|2
  }
- output/summary.md containing a brief summary with: the counts per severity, the final score, grade, and exit code. Use relative file paths in any examples.

Important:
- Only use the detection and scoring rules defined above (do not add extra categories).
- Treat input/WORKSPACE as read-only and do not modify anything under input/.
- All file paths in findings must be relative to input/WORKSPACE (e.g., "skills/evil-skill/evil.py").

Please read all files under input/WORKSPACE/ before auditing. Then produce the three outputs: output/.marshal-policy.json, output/audit.json, and output/summary.md.
